name: Sierra de Burete - Doña Inés - Caravaca de la Cruz  — circular from Cehegín
name_original: Sierra de Burete - Doña Inés - Caravaca de la Cruz  — circular desde Cehegín
region_intro: Road cycling around Cehegín offers diverse terrain, from challenging mountainous areas to gentle plains and river valleys. The region features high peaks such as Sierra de Lavia and Sierra de Burete, alongside lower elevations like Cabezos de la Fuente del Pintor. Cyclists can explore fertile plains and river valleys, including the Argos and Quípar rivers. A notable feature is the Vía Verde, a traffic-free path suitable for various cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Cehegín The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Burete - Doña Inés - Caravaca de la Cruz — circular from Cehegín, a 36.7 miles (59.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Sierra de Burete and connects to Caravaca de la Cruz. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Caravaca de la Cruz – Moratalla loop from Cehegín, a moderate 34.0 miles (54.8 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through the region, passing through Caravaca de la Cruz and Moratalla. Local road cyclists also love the Fuentes del Marqués – Castillo de Don Fabio loop from Cehegín, a 62.4 miles (100.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Cehegín is defined by mountainous areas, fertile plains, and river valleys, including the Vía Verde. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Cehegín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1,950 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cehegín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cehegín?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes around Cehegín, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 107 routes in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cehegín?
A: The best times for road cycling in Cehegín are typically between February and June, and then again from September to November. These periods avoid the intense summer heat, offering more pleasant temperatures for riding.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Cehegín?
A: Cehegín offers a diverse terrain for road cycling, ranging from challenging mountainous areas with peaks like Sierra de Lavia and Sierra de Burete, to more gentle mountainous elevations, fertile plains, and scenic river valleys. This variety ensures routes suitable for different abilities and preferences.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Cehegín offers several routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40318117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Arch of Cehegín loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22.9 km route that provides a good introduction to the area's cycling. In total, there are 7 easy routes available.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Cehegín has 35 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes del Marqués – Castillo de Don Fabio loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt;, a 100.3 km trail with significant elevation gain, typically completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Cehegín?
A: The road cycling routes in Cehegín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic valley rides, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Cehegín?
A: Yes, many routes around Cehegín are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009751049/sierra-de-burete-dona-ines-caravaca-de-la-cruz-circular-from-cehegin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Burete - Doña Inés - Caravaca de la Cruz — circular from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caravaca de la Cruz – Moratalla loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints or natural attractions?
A: Yes, Cehegín's routes often pass through areas of natural beauty. You can find routes that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes del Marqués&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument, or near the ecological areas around the Argos and Quípar rivers. The region's varied topography also provides numerous scenic vistas.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Cehegín is rich in history. Many routes offer glimpses of historical sites. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2724114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Don Fabio&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;. The old town of Cehegín itself is a Historical-Artistic Ensemble with charming streets and ancient buildings.

Q: Is it possible to cycle on the Vía Verde around Cehegín?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde is a significant attraction for cyclists in the region. This former railway line has been transformed into a well-maintained, traffic-free path connecting Cehegín with Caravaca and Murcia, making it ideal for leisurely rides and exploring the landscape without vehicle traffic.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features accessible by road bike?
A: While primarily a road cycling guide, some routes may bring you close to natural water features. The region is home to the Argos and Quípar rivers, and highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Usero&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural attractions in the wider area, though direct road bike access to the falls themselves may vary.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Cehegín?
A: For families, the Vía Verde offers a safe and traffic-free environment, which is ideal for cycling with children. While specific family-focused road routes are not detailed, the easier routes around Cehegín, particularly those on flatter terrain or dedicated paths, can be suitable for families.
tips: Located near the town of Cehegín, on the Rambla del Paraíso, we find this curious aqueduct, a work of modern times, built in all probability on Roman remains.
Beautiful prints that we will find rolling along the Murcian highway RM-504 during our route through the Sierra de Burete.
